title: New Kid On The Block

jokes.text: D'oh! [Homer's signature exclamation after crashing car]
jokes.setup: Homer crashes car in opening credits
jokes.punchline: His iconic frustrated exclamation
jokes.explanation: Homer's signature catchphrase - perfect character integration and maximum quotability, though not original by this point in the series

jokes.text: Well, that's a tough one, 'cause I did the deed with Uta, Candy and Shasta.
jokes.setup: Game show asking which woman said something romantic
jokes.punchline: Contestant casually admits to sleeping with multiple women with ridiculous names
jokes.explanation: Satirizes trashy daytime TV with absurd contestant behavior and silly names

jokes.text: But I'm gonna have to go with Shasta... 'cause she liked makin' bacon on the beach.
jokes.setup: Continuation of game show scenario
jokes.punchline: Euphemistic wordplay about sexual activity
jokes.explanation: Crude double entendre that fits the trashy TV parody but lacks sophistication

jokes.text: Ahhh, bacon. [Homer's dreamy response to hearing 'bacon']
jokes.setup: TV character mentions 'making bacon'
jokes.punchline: Homer completely misses the sexual innuendo and just thinks about actual bacon
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Homer's food obsession makes him completely oblivious to sexual content, excellent economy

jokes.text: could you please wear pants? - Mmm, no.
jokes.setup: Neighbor politely asks Homer to wear pants when visible
jokes.punchline: Homer's matter-of-fact refusal
jokes.explanation: Homer's casual selfishness delivered with perfect deadpan economy

jokes.text: could you please take in your jack-o'-lanterns from past Halloweens? - Mmm, no.
jokes.setup: Second polite request from neighbor
jokes.punchline: Identical refusal pattern, revealing Homer's lazy procrastination
jokes.explanation: Running gag of refusals, plus absurd detail about multiple years of pumpkins

jokes.text: Wha-- Hey, moose! Shoo! Yeah, I'm talkin' to you! Get off my lawn, now!
jokes.setup: Neighbor complains about garbage attracting wildlife
jokes.punchline: A literal moose on Homer's lawn, which he addresses like a neighborhood kid
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from complaint to absurd reality, with Homer treating dangerous wildlife like a minor nuisance

jokes.text: Okay, Homer, stay calm. just quietly get this stuff inside your house.
jokes.setup: Homer finds garbage scattered on lawn
jokes.punchline: His solution is to hide the evidence rather than clean up properly
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer logic - avoiding the real problem with a worse solution

jokes.text: Maybe I'm not getting enough... estrogen.
jokes.setup: Homer considers taking expired medicine
jokes.punchline: His completely wrong understanding of what estrogen does
jokes.explanation: Homer's medical ignorance creates absurd self-diagnosis moment

jokes.text: But I think I'm allergic to seafood. The last time I ate shrimp, my throat closed up, and I went into convulsions. - Mmm, shrimp.
jokes.setup: Marge describes serious allergic reaction to seafood
jokes.punchline: Homer completely ignores the danger and focuses on how good shrimp sounds
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - food obsession overrides all concern for Marge's health

jokes.text: Mmm, shrimp.
jokes.setup: Marge describing her allergic reaction to shrimp
jokes.punchline: Homer's oblivious food lust despite the medical emergency
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer food obsession overriding all other concerns, perfectly timed

jokes.text: Now, I don't know much about haggling or bargaining... so why don't we just agree to pay whatever the Winfields want?
jokes.setup: Discussion about negotiating house sale
jokes.punchline: Homer admits incompetence then suggests worst possible negotiating strategy
jokes.explanation: Homer's complete lack of business sense presented as helpful suggestion

jokes.text: This is where the Winfields hid their mutated son. - Bart, the Winfields didn't have a son. - See that sock over there? It was his only friend.
jokes.setup: Bart tells scary story about neighbors' basement
jokes.punchline: Absurd detail about sock being mutated son's only friend
jokes.explanation: Classic Bart storytelling with absurd, darkly comic details that feel genuinely disturbing

jokes.text: [Thinking] She's beautiful. Say something clever. - I fell on my bottom. - [Thinking] D'oh!
jokes.setup: Bart wants to impress new neighbor girl
jokes.punchline: His attempt at clever conversation is embarrassingly childish
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/payoff structure showing gap between Bart's intentions and abilities

jokes.text: My upbringing was painfully strict, ma'am.
jokes.setup: Marge compliments Laura's politeness
jokes.punchline: Laura's deadpan admission that it came from harsh parenting
jokes.explanation: Subverts expectation that politeness comes from good upbringing with dark truth

jokes.text: especially after that Time cover story, 'America's Worst City.' - You could see our house in that photo.
jokes.setup: New neighbor mentions negative magazine coverage of Springfield
jokes.punchline: Homer proudly identifies their house in the photo illustrating the city's awfulness
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke about the show's premise while revealing Homer's complete lack of shame

jokes.text: They're so sweet when you marry them. But soon, it's just career, career, career.
jokes.setup: Divorced woman explaining her situation
jokes.punchline: Bitter commentary delivered with false sweetness
jokes.explanation: Standard divorced spouse complaint, predictable but well-delivered

jokes.text: My hammock! Do you understand? Mine! Don't look that way!
jokes.setup: Serious conversation about divorce happening
jokes.punchline: Homer's completely inappropriate territorial outburst about lawn furniture
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - completely missing emotional context for petty material concerns

jokes.text: How'd you like a Hertz donut? - Hurts, don't it?
jokes.setup: Bart offers Laura a 'Hertz donut'
jokes.punchline: Punches her and reveals the pun
jokes.explanation: Classic schoolyard prank joke - low originality but perfect for Bart's character

jokes.text: Hey, baby! How about puttin' your finger in my ear? - Well, I don't know. Your boyfriend looks like the jealous type.
jokes.setup: Bullies hitting on Laura
jokes.punchline: Laura cleverly redirects their aggression toward each other
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment showing Laura's intelligence and quick thinking

jokes.text: Hey, what the-- - That chick's messin' with our minds. Let's get out of here.
jokes.setup: Laura's manipulation causes confusion
jokes.punchline: Bullies retreat in confusion, recognizing they've been outsmarted
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to Laura's manipulation, showing even bullies know when they're beaten

jokes.text: Oh, man. You're gonna be rich! [spits in hand] There's your mansion. There's the tennis court. And there's the swimming pool.
jokes.setup: Laura offers to tell Bart's fortune
jokes.punchline: She spits in her hand and points to different saliva globs as luxury items
jokes.explanation: Gross-out humor that's creative and shows Laura's playful manipulation of Bart

jokes.text: Oh, man. You're gonna be rich! There's your mansion. There's the tennis court. And there's the swimming pool.
jokes.setup: Laura pretending to tell Bart's fortune
jokes.punchline: She spits to create the 'swimming pool' in his palm
jokes.explanation: Cute visual gag that works as flirtation between the characters

jokes.text: I'll never wash this hand again.
jokes.setup: Laura spits in Bart's hand for 'fortune telling'
jokes.punchline: Bart treasures the gross experience because of his crush
jokes.explanation: Classic young crush behavior - finding romantic significance in disgusting acts

jokes.text: Sorry, Lise. I no longer control the hand. The hand controls me!
jokes.setup: Lisa wants Bart to wash his hand
jokes.punchline: Bart claims mystical possession by his unwashed hand
jokes.explanation: Escalates the gross hand joke with absurd mystical justification

jokes.text: Sorry, this isn't Abbie. This is her sister. I look after her now.
jokes.setup: Someone calls asking for a babysitter
jokes.punchline: Implies something terrible happened to the original babysitter
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that lets imagination fill in disturbing backstory

jokes.text: I wore my extra-loose pants for nothing. Nothing!
jokes.setup: All-you-can-eat seafood plan falls through
jokes.punchline: Homer's dramatic despair over wasted eating preparation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer food obsession moment - the preparation ritual matters as much as the eating

jokes.text: Oh, I get it. 'Bart, the babysitter's here.' 'Let me tuck you in.'
jokes.setup: Suggestion that Laura could babysit
jokes.punchline: Bart fantasizes about romantic scenarios disguised as babysitting
jokes.explanation: Awkward pre-teen romantic fantasy with appropriately cringey delivery

jokes.text: Maybe it's time I wash my hand.
jokes.setup: Bart realizes Laura will be coming over
jokes.punchline: Suddenly practical considerations outweigh romantic sentiment
jokes.explanation: Nice callback showing even Bart has some social awareness limits

jokes.text: That's it! Whoo-hoo! I'm glad one of us remembered. That could have been embarrassing.
jokes.setup: Marge mentions being divorced as sensitive topic
jokes.punchline: Homer celebrates remembering something he clearly forgot
jokes.explanation: Homer's lack of social awareness presented as helpful competence

jokes.text: After all, Homer, I do have the normal appetites.
jokes.setup: Discussion about setting up dates
jokes.punchline: Euphemistic reference to sexual needs
jokes.explanation: Awkward adult conversation that creates discomfort through euphemism

jokes.text: just let me make sure we're not talking about food. - I'm not. - Right. Me neither. We're talking about sex, right? - Right.
jokes.setup: Euphemistic conversation about 'appetites'
jokes.punchline: Homer's need to explicitly clarify they're discussing sex, not food
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - his food obsession makes him need clarification about obvious euphemisms

jokes.text: Hey, sometimes a guy just likes his skin to look its yellowest.
jokes.setup: Lisa questions why Bart is taking a bath
jokes.punchline: Reference to The Simpsons' distinctive yellow character design
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-joke acknowledging the show's visual style while staying in character

jokes.text: Sometimes I think you want to fail! Shut up. just shut up!
jokes.setup: TV drama playing in background
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic dialogue typical of soap operas
jokes.explanation: Generic soap opera parody without much specificity or wit

jokes.text: Take your kaftab'Sounieh and dip it in the labneh. Now, that is good labneh.
jokes.setup: Laura serves exotic foreign food
jokes.punchline: Made-up foreign food names that sound authentic but absurd
jokes.explanation: Satirizes exotic food culture with fake but plausible-sounding dishes

jokes.text: We used to eat this when my dad was stationed in 'Vandihar.'
jokes.setup: Laura serving ethnic food
jokes.punchline: Mispronunciation of Kandahar as 'Vandihar'
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay on geographic name - shows Laura's worldly background

jokes.text: What about the bread? Does that have much fish in it? - Yes.
jokes.setup: Marge tries to find non-seafood options at seafood restaurant
jokes.punchline: Even bread contains fish at this extreme seafood establishment
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of restaurant's seafood obsession to absurd extremes

jokes.text: Well, I have some Tic Tacs in my purse-- - Excellent choice.
jokes.setup: Marge mentions breath mints as last resort
jokes.punchline: Waiter treats mints as legitimate menu item
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response that maintains restaurant's professional facade despite absurdity

jokes.text: just follow me. Put your hand on my hip. [Voice Cracking] Okay.
jokes.setup: Laura teaches Bart to dance
jokes.punchline: Bart's voice cracks from pubescent nervousness at physical contact
jokes.explanation: Authentic pre-teen awkwardness with perfect physical comedy timing

jokes.text: That man ate all our shrimp and two plastic lobsters! 'Tis no man. 'Tis a remorseless eating machine. Arr!
jokes.setup: Restaurant staff discusses Homer's eating
jokes.punchline: Captain's dramatic description escalates to eating inedible decorations
jokes.explanation: Great character voice for the sea captain with escalating absurdity of Homer's appetite

jokes.text: I'm like that guy, that Spanish guy. You know, he fought the windmills. - Don Quixote? - No, that's not it. What's his name? 'The Man of La Mancha.' - Don Quixote. - No!
jokes.setup: Homer tries to make literary reference
jokes.punchline: His stubborn refusal to accept correct answer shows intellectual insecurity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - intellectual pretension combined with stubborn ignorance

jokes.text: Mr. Simpson, this is the most blatant case of fraudulent advertising since my suit against the film The Neverending Story.
jokes.setup: Lawyer describes Homer's case as serious fraud
jokes.punchline: Compares it to suing a movie for having an ending despite its title
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic that's both character-perfect for Lionel Hutz and genuinely clever wordplay

jokes.text: Homer, I don't use the word 'hero' very often. But you are the greatest hero in American history.
jokes.setup: Hutz praising Homer
jokes.punchline: Absurd escalation from rarely using 'hero' to greatest in American history
jokes.explanation: Great Hutz escalation showing his tendency toward ridiculous hyperbole

jokes.text: Homer, I don't use the word 'hero' very often. But you are the greatest hero in American history.
jokes.setup: Lawyer claims he doesn't use 'hero' lightly
jokes.punchline: Immediately gives the most extreme hero designation possible
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation showing Hutz's over-the-top legal rhetoric and lack of perspective

jokes.text: Hey, can your grandfather do this? [Karate Yells] - Oh!
jokes.setup: Old man tries to impress Bart with karate
jokes.punchline: Presumably hurts himself attempting martial arts
jokes.explanation: Standard old person physical comedy without much originality

jokes.text: I had a crush on the oldest woman. 120 years old, she was. Here's a picture of her delivering Eubie Blake.
jokes.setup: Grandpa tells story about crush on older woman
jokes.punchline: Absurdly old age and historical anachronism
jokes.explanation: Classic Grandpa Simpson rambling with historical absurdity and perfect character voice

jokes.text: I wore a 15-pound beard of bees for that woman, but it just wasn't enough.
jokes.setup: Grandpa explains what happened with his crush
jokes.punchline: Absurd romantic gesture that's both extreme and pathetic
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd image that's both specific and completely unexpected - classic Simpsons surrealism

jokes.text: Ooh! 'Mostly Mozart' is in town.
jokes.setup: Homer pretends to read cultural calendar to avoid sex talk
jokes.punchline: His choice of highbrow cultural event is hilariously out of character
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer avoidance technique with incongruous cultural reference

jokes.text: [Monkeys Chittering] Zookeeper! Zookeeper! Those two monkeys are killing each other! - They're having sex. - Oh?
jokes.setup: Young Homer's misunderstanding of monkey behavior
jokes.punchline: Innocent misinterpretation of sexual activity as violence
jokes.explanation: Classic sex education avoidance joke with good setup/payoff structure

jokes.text: Son, a woman is a lot like, um... a refrigerator. They're about six feet tall, 300 pounds. They make ice and-- Oh, wait a minute. Actually, a woman is more like a beer.
jokes.setup: Homer attempts fatherly wisdom about women
jokes.punchline: His analogy is literally about appliances, showing complete cluelessness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer parenting moment - completely missing the point with literal-minded analogies

jokes.text: They smell good. They look good. You'd step over your own mother just to get one! But you can't stop at one. You wanna drink another woman.
jokes.setup: Homer's beer analogy for women continues
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate sexual advice disguised as beer appreciation
jokes.explanation: Escalation of terrible parenting advice showing Homer's priorities and complete lack of wisdom

jokes.text: You know that dead body they found behind the mayor's house? - Jimbo killed him? - No, but he poked him with a stick.
jokes.setup: Laura describing Jimbo's 'outlaw' status with ominous buildup
jokes.punchline: Anticlimactic revelation of mild vandalism instead of murder
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection that perfectly deflates the 'dangerous rebel' mystique with childish reality

jokes.text: You know that dead body they found behind the mayor's house? - Jimbo killed him? - No, but he poked him with a stick.
jokes.setup: Laura explains Jimbo's criminal reputation
jokes.punchline: Anticlimactic revelation that his 'crime' was just juvenile curiosity
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that deflates tough guy reputation with childish reality

jokes.text: [Echoing] Just a kid. Just a kid.
jokes.setup: Laura dismisses Bart as 'just a kid' to Jimbo
jokes.punchline: Bart's internal echo of the hurtful dismissal
jokes.explanation: Effective use of echo effect to show emotional impact on Bart

jokes.text: I married her! [Homer laughing at his own joke]
jokes.setup: Bart asks about pursuing girl who likes someone beneath her
jokes.punchline: Homer jokes that he married above his level
jokes.explanation: Self-deprecating humor that shows Homer's rare moment of self-awareness

jokes.text: 'Twas a moonless night, dark as pitch... when out of the mist came a beast more stomach than man.
jokes.setup: Captain testifies about Homer's eating
jokes.punchline: Epic storytelling style for describing customer behavior
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character voice turning mundane restaurant incident into epic tale

jokes.text: 18,000 letters all addressed to 'Santa Claus.' - You want 'The People of Springfield versus Kris Kringle.' That's next door.
jokes.setup: Wrong evidence brought to courtroom
jokes.punchline: Reveals there's actually a lawsuit against Santa Claus
jokes.explanation: Absurd world-building that suggests Springfield regularly sues fictional characters

jokes.text: 18,000 letters all addressed to 'Santa Claus.' - You want 'The People of Springfield versus Kris Kringle.' That's next door.
jokes.setup: Evidence being brought into wrong courtroom
jokes.punchline: Revelation of even more ridiculous lawsuit next door
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist moment suggesting Springfield residents sue Santa Claus. Great world-building humor.

jokes.text: Mrs. Simpson, isn't it true your husband once consumed a 10-pound bag of flour when no other food was available? - Yes, but it was--
jokes.setup: Establishing Homer's eating habits in court
jokes.punchline: Absurd eating behavior that Marge tries to defend
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Homer's eating extremes with Marge's loyal but futile defense

jokes.text: We drove around until 3:00 a.m. looking for another all-you-can-eat fish restaurant. - And when you couldn't find one? - We went fishing. [Sobbing]
jokes.setup: Marge describes post-restaurant activities
jokes.punchline: Escalating desperation leads to literal fishing
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation showing Homer's food obsession driving family to extreme measures

jokes.text: Come see 'Bottomless Pete,' nature's cruelest mistake. - Oh, he's hideous! - I heard they shaved a gorilla.
jokes.setup: Freak show advertising
jokes.punchline: Crowd's cruel commentary on the performer
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about exploitation that's more uncomfortable than funny

jokes.text: No, this is... Bo's Cavern.
jokes.setup: Someone uses coupon for free beer at Moe's Tavern
jokes.punchline: Moe tries to avoid honoring coupon with weak name change
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe character moment - cheap scheme with minimal effort

jokes.text: Uh, Amanda Huggenkiss! Hey, I'm looking for Amanda Huggenkiss! Why can't I find Amanda Huggenkiss?
jokes.setup: Moe calls out name from prank call
jokes.punchline: Sexual innuendo in fake name when said aloud
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons prank call with good wordplay structure

jokes.text: Maybe your standards are too high.
jokes.setup: Moe's frustrated search for 'Amanda Huggenkiss'
jokes.punchline: Barney's advice treats it as legitimate romantic pursuit
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that extends the joke with perfect Barney logic

jokes.text: My name is Jimbo Jones, and I live at 1094 Evergreen Terrace.
jokes.setup: Prank call leads to Moe's threats
jokes.punchline: Bart gives Jimbo's real address for revenge
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that sets up Bart's revenge plot with clever misdirection

jokes.text: What kind of pathetic drunk Do you take me for? [Gasps] Somebody spilled beer in this ashtray. [Slurps Loudly] Ahh.
jokes.setup: Barney indignantly defends his character
jokes.punchline: Immediately proves Moe right by drinking spilled beer from ashtray
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - immediate contradiction of self-defense with disgusting behavior

jokes.text: I wasn't really gonna kill ya. I was just gonna cut ya. [Sobbing Continues]
jokes.setup: Moe confronts terrified Jimbo
jokes.punchline: His attempt at reassurance is still threatening
jokes.explanation: Dark humor showing Moe's skewed sense of mercy and intimidation

jokes.text: You know, if you were only old enough to grow a bad teenage mustache... I'd go out with you in a second.
jokes.setup: Laura acknowledges Bart was right about Jimbo
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment that highlights age gap impossibility
jokes.explanation: Bittersweet moment that acknowledges Bart's maturity while emphasizing the impossibility

jokes.text: Ivana Tinkle. just a sec. Ivana Tinkle. Ivana Tinkle! All right, everybody, put down your glasses. Ivana Tinkle.
jokes.setup: Another prank call to Moe's
jokes.punchline: Bathroom humor pun when said aloud
jokes.explanation: Classic prank call format with juvenile but effective wordplay

jokes.text: Hello, I'd like to speak to Miss Tinkle, first name... Ivana. Ivana Tinkle. just a sec. Ivana Tinkle. Ivana Tinkle! All right, everybody, put down your glasses. Ivana Tinkle.
jokes.setup: Another prank call with bathroom humor name
jokes.punchline: Moe's escalating desperation to find the fake person
jokes.explanation: Standard juvenile prank call, less clever than earlier ones but provides closure to the bit

tmdb_overview: Bart has a crush on his new neighbor, a teenage girl who only has eyes for Jimbo Jones. Bart sets out to prove that Jimbo is not as tough as he seems.
insight: New Kids parody sustains 70 jokes across 22 minutes, anchored by character escalation.
